channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull rewrites dinosaur evolution and [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo region develop locally-controlled, long-term scientific monitoring infrastructure using accessible methodologies without reproducing extractive research models? Topics include: Environmental DNA methodology for monitoring aquatic species without capture or disturbance, Indigenous data sovereignty and governance requirements for research conducted on traditional territories, Acoustic ecology and plant responses to environmental sound in forest regeneration contexts, Old-growth forests as scientific archives containing climate and cosmic event records, Community foundations and endowment models as sustainable funding vehicles for long-term ecological monitoring.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] HANSEN: Lac La Hache Community Club president steps up to run bingo and [Williams Lake Tribune] 2025 Williams Lake Fire Department recruits earn Live Fire Interior Attack certification, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What combination of external recognition, cultural transmission, and civic investment creates sustainable rural community resilience, and how do communities choose what to anchor their identity to? Topics include: The biomass award as external validation of local problem-solving, Cultural transmission through youth fiddle society and its relationship to retention, Leadership succession and bench depth in volunteer organizations, High-infrastructure anchors (junior hockey) versus low-infrastructure sustainable activities, The distinction between sustaining activities and anchoring community identity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Women's Centre

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Resilience.org] Environmental protection depends on more than regulation and [Williams Lake Tribune] Help B.C. communities recover from wildfires 1 beer at a time, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technology tools and targeted institutional interventions meaningfully improve wildfire preparedness and emergency survival readiness in rural Cariboo communities? Topics include: Satellite communicator technology accessibility and pricing for rural populations, AI-assisted emergency planning tools and demographic limitations, Interoperability and standards gaps in emergency communication devices, Community social infrastructure versus top-down institutional programs, Role of local institutions (credit unions, search and rescue, Regional Districts) in building preparedness.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Scout Island Nature Centre

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] BC’s New Chief Treaty Commissioner on the Path Forward and [Global News] Manitoba’s nursing regulator apologizes for discrimination against Indigenous Peoples, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities build internal technical capacity to implement data sovereignty frameworks, and what is the role of long-term human capital development versus short-term institutional solutions? Topics include: Indspire scholarship pipeline and long-term capacity building, UVic digital collections project and governance authority over digitization, FirstVoices as model for community-controlled digital infrastructure, Institutional hiring practices and the difference between advisory roles and decision-making authority, Funding models and structural durability: government dependency versus charitable sector diversification.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Indian Band

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Tom's Hardware] Louis Rossmann tells 3D printer maker Bambu Lab to ‘Go (Bleep) yourself’ over its threatened lawsuit against enthusiast — Right to Repair advocate offers to pay the legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer and [Hackaday] Trying to Fix a GoPro Hero 10 With No Camera Input Issue, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities build sustainable, maintainable technology infrastructure when manufacturers increasingly restrict ownership rights and technical expertise is geographically concentrated? Topics include: Bambu Lab lawsuit and proprietary ecosystem lock-in versus right-to-repair, Hybrid DIY/commercial manufacturing models for complex systems, Rural technology equity and geographic access barriers, Federal and provincial right-to-repair legislation landscape, Tool-lending libraries and institutional infrastructure for rural tech access.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Regional District Library

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Fuelled by AI, Micron's $50 billion chip factory in Idaho will use billions of litres of water every year — but the US tech giant stays silent on where that new water will come from and [TechRadar] OpenAI's ‘Trusted Contact’ feature for ChatGPT users in crisis is a sign AI is becoming something much more personal, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can BC deploy AI-driven mineral exploration technology responsibly, or must governance capacity be built before the technology accelerates discovery? Topics include: AI mineral exploration technology and KoBold Metals' competitive advantages, Institutional capacity gaps in provincial and Indigenous consultation and assessment processes, Data sovereignty and public value capture from publicly-funded geological data, Nation-level technical capacity building as a structural intervention in resource governance, Sequencing of technology deployment versus governance system readiness.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Food Policy Council

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Business] James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Major Parts of Vox Media and [Engadget] Spotify now lets AI agents like OpenClaw generate personal podcasts, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural cultural institutions build sustainable, discoverable storytelling capacity in an environment flooded with AI-generated content while maintaining community control and authentic relationships? Topics include: AI-generated content's impact on discoverability for authentic rural cultural storytelling, Governance models balancing community control with visibility (Mukurtu tiered-access system), Funding models for digital capacity at small cultural institutions (provincial arts funding vs. co-op models), Technology's relationship to community vitality (downstream not upstream), Place-specific authenticity as defense against synthetic content and media pressure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Museum of the Cariboo Chilcotin

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Top Stories] New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestation. And Rewards of Protection. and [ScienceDaily] Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can recent scientific discoveries about conserved mechanisms in regeneration, evolution, and quantum systems translate into actionable improvements for rural communities like the Cariboo? Topics include: Gene therapy and regenerative medicine accessibility in rural communities, Evolutionary conservation of genetic expression mechanisms and ecological succession prediction, Indigenous data sovereignty and co-investigator research partnerships with universities, Monitoring commons governance and the role of temporal sequence in system dynamics, Analytical capacity as the primary bottleneck rather than data availability or discovery.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Habitat for Humanity Cariboo

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] New exhibitions at Williams Lake Station House Gallery celebrate B.C.’s wildlife, natural beauty and [Williams Lake Tribune] Walk held from Williams Lake to Tl’esqox First Nation for Sabrina Rosette, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What actually holds a rural community together, and how can external programs and policymakers effectively support the social and institutional infrastructure that enables this resilience? Topics include: The role of unglamorous infrastructure maintenance decisions in rural community resilience, Institutional leadership continuity and its non-transferable knowledge in rural development bodies, Low-barrier integrated youth mental health service models and funding sustainability, Indigenous-led economic development and cultural tourism as alternative rural capacity-building, The gap between program funding and workforce retention capacity in rural service delivery.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: CMHA Cariboo Chilcotin

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New exhibitions at Williams Lake Sta...

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] New exhibitions at Williams Lake Station House Gallery celebrate B.C.’s wildlife, natural beauty and [Williams Lake Tribune] Walk held from Williams Lake to Tl’esqox First Nation for Sabrina Rosette, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What actually holds a rural community together, and how can external programs and policymakers effectively support the social and institutional infrastructure that enables this resilience? Topics include: The role of unglamorous infrastructure maintenance decisions in rural community resilience, Institutional leadership continuity and its non-transferable knowledge in rural development bodies, Low-barrier integrated youth mental health service models and funding sustainability, Indigenous-led economic development and cultural tourism as alternative rural capacity-building, The gap between program funding and workforce retention capacity in rural service delivery.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: CMHA Cariboo Chilcotin

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Day-use passes available for three popular parks, seasonal closures at Pipi7íyekw/Joffre Lakes Park and [The Tyee] Diary of an Accidental Birder, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Does BC's new lightning detection technology funding represent a meaningful advance in wildfire prevention, or does it risk becoming a demonstration project without the governance structure and procurement commitment to achieve real operational impact? Topics include: Lightning detection technology and its role in reducing detection-to-initial-attack timeframes, Fuel mapping data currency and its impact on ignition probability modeling accuracy, Drone-based thermal surveillance capabilities and Transport Canada BVLOS regulatory constraints, Indigenous Nation governance and territorial protocols in fire detection system deployment, Procurement strategy versus grant funding as mechanisms for technology sector viability and operational scaling.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Scout Island Nature Centre
